mechanism_narrative: A high and stable federal funds rate increases the discount rate for corporate earnings and provides a competitive, low-risk return alternative via cash deposits, leading to outflows from equities and depressing S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% is the highest level in over 15 years as of 2023-12-15.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a very tight labor market, which fuels wage inflation, compresses corporate profit margins, and increases the likelihood of further monetary tightening, all of which are negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 3.7%, meeting the trigger condition.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) in periods when UNRATE < 4.0% compared to periods when UNRATE >= 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated while the central bank has already raised rates significantly (FEDFUNDS), it creates policy uncertainty. This interaction signals that the Fed may be forced into more aggressive, growth-damaging hikes, which negatively impacts equity risk premia.
evidence_refs: fact:Current conditions meet the trigger (CPI YoY ~3.1%, FEDFUNDS=5.33%).
failure_condition: A regression model shows the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not a statistically significant negative predictor of 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which is the primary driver of corporate revenues. Positive momentum in this series leads to upward revisions in earnings estimates and boosts equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending has remained resilient despite higher rates.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in RSAFS (adjusted for inflation) shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) during a high interest rate regime signals that tight monetary policy is successfully slowing the industrial economy, a leading indicator of a broader recession and earnings decline, thus negatively impacting equities.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has shown signs of stagnation/decline in recent months.
failure_condition: Periods of contracting INDPRO when FEDFUNDS > 4% do not lead to statistically lower 60-day returns than periods of contracting INDPRO when FEDFUNDS < 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in the rate of job creation (PAYEMS) is a high-frequency leading indicator of a cooling economy. This negatively impacts short-term market sentiment as investors anticipate lower future growth, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The pace of job growth has been moderating throughout 2023.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following payroll reports below the trigger threshold are not statistically lower (p > 0.05) than returns following reports above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the most direct measure of aggregate economic health. Positive surprises or strong prints confirm a robust economy, supporting corporate earnings and justifying higher equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:The Q3 2023 GDP growth was exceptionally strong, surprising expectations.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly real GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.05 over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a classic leading indicator of economic slowdown, as the housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates and consumer confidence. This weakness presages broader economic trouble and negatively affects equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average shows a month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity has been suppressed by high mortgage rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a full business cycle.
